channel.title: Ninad Malvankar — Engineering Articles
channel.description: Engineering leadership, system design, and architecture insights by Ninad Malvankar, Architect at Upstox. AWS Certified cloud architect with 12+ years in FinTech platform engineering.

item.title: How a Bad Webpack Config Can Silently Destroy Frontend Performance
item.description: Misconfigured bundlers don't always throw errors — sometimes they just make your app slower in ways that are hard to detect. A deep dive into the subtle ways webpack config mistakes can silently kill frontend performance.
item.content:encoded:
Misconfigured bundlers don't always throw errors — sometimes they just make your app slower in ways that are hard to detect. A deep dive into the subtle ways webpack config mistakes can silently kill frontend performance.

Read the full article on Medium.
item.dc:creator: Ninad Malvankar

item.title: Spring Security Meets Java 21: A Closer Look at Firewall Controls
item.description: Many backend engineers upgrade to the latest Java and Spring versions for performance gains — but overlook deeper security implications. A closer look at how Spring Security's firewall controls behave in a Java 21 environment.
item.content:encoded:
Many backend engineers upgrade to the latest Java and Spring versions for performance gains — but overlook deeper security implications. A closer look at how Spring Security's firewall controls behave in a Java 21 environment.

Read the full article on Medium.
item.dc:creator: Ninad Malvankar

item.title: What Happens When You Outgrow Mentorship? Lessons from a Principal Engineer
item.description: Outgrowing formal mentorship can feel isolating. Treating yourself like a system — running personal retrospectives each quarter and building your own feedback loop — is how growth continues at the principal level.
item.content:encoded:
Outgrowing formal mentorship can feel isolating. Treating yourself like a system — running personal retrospectives each quarter and building your own feedback loop — is how growth continues at the principal level.

Read the full article on Medium.
item.dc:creator: Ninad Malvankar

item.title: The Role of a Principal Engineer — Leadership Without Authority
item.description: Embracing ambiguity, driving clarity, and working through influence — not authority. It's not about how much code you write, it's about how many people you help move faster, safer, and smarter.
item.content:encoded:
Embracing ambiguity, driving clarity, and working through influence — not authority. It's not about how much code you write, it's about how many people you help move faster, safer, and smarter.

Read the full article on Medium.
item.dc:creator: Ninad Malvankar
